addresses: latent regression risk for the 9 silently-PASS bridges; with curl now LIVE per omega-bridge-2..5, schema drift can finally surface instead of corrupting downstream silently.

fingerprint_algorithm.primary: jq -r 'keys | sort | join("|")' on top-level object
fingerprint_algorithm.array_handling: jq inspects first element; if object, prefixes with '__array__:' and uses its keys; otherwise '__array_of_<type>__'
fingerprint_algorithm.fallback_when_no_jq: grep -oE '"<word>":' over body | sed | sort -u | tr '\n' '|' (crude — picks nested keys too — but deterministic and catches gross drift)
fingerprint_algorithm.hash_algo: sha1, truncated to first 16 hex chars (matches existing _url_hash convention)
what_it_does_NOT_catch: type changes within an existing field (string->int, object->array)
value semantics changes with stable keys
fingerprint_algorithm.rationale: cheap + deterministic + jq-or-grep portable; first-line defence; full schema validation deferred.

exit_codes.0: BASELINE_RECORDED (first run for bridge) OR MATCH (fp == baseline)
exit_codes.1: DRIFT_DETECTED (fp != baseline; candidate row written, baseline preserved)
exit_codes.2: FETCH_FAIL or NON_JSON_BODY or missing args

new_subcommand.stderr_diagnostics: raw 66 compliant (reason+fix lines on each failure)
new_subcommand.side_effects: appends to baseline TSV; on drift writes 'candidate:<fp>' row, never overwrites baseline (operator-approved rotation)

baseline_path.candidate_marker: candidate:<fp> in column 2 (looked up via awk filter '$2 !~ /^candidate:/')

spot_test_results.harness: preseeded /tmp/omega_bridge_6_test/cache via _url_hash(URL)+.body file; URL='https://example.invalid/codata_v1' (NEVER hit network — cache hit path only).

test_3_DRIFT_DETECTED.input_body: {"alpha_inverse": 137.035999177, "uncertainty": 0.000000021, "year": 2022} (renamed alpha_inv → alpha_inverse — synthetic upstream API rename)
test_3_DRIFT_DETECTED.tsv_after: baseline row preserved; new candidate row appended: codata_test\tcandidate:a20551f6039f158f\t2026-04-26T07:11:58Z

recommendation_for_bridge_health_integra.reason: this cycle delivers the helper primitive (fingerprint-check) plus baseline storage — keeping change surface tight to bridge_helper.sh per ω-cycle constraint. bridge_health.sh integration involves: (a) URL-discovery per bridge (currently each .hexa hardcodes its own), (b) policy on first-run BASELINE_RECORDED (silent-pass vs WARN), (c) summary aggregation in the existing PASS/FAIL/WARN report.

proposed_omega_bridge_7_design.step_1: add a per-bridge URL/TTL manifest at state/bridge_endpoints.tsv (bridge\turl\tttl) — sourced once by bridge_health.sh
proposed_omega_bridge_7_design.step_2: add --fingerprint-check flag to bridge_health.sh; iterate manifest, invoke 'bash bridge_helper.sh fingerprint-check $bridge $url $ttl 1', collect status lines
proposed_omega_bridge_7_design.step_3: summary section: 'schema drift: <n> DRIFT, <n> NEW_BASELINE, <n> MATCH, <n> NON_JSON, <n> SKIP'; DRIFT entries surface in WARN tier
proposed_omega_bridge_7_design.step_4: operator approval workflow: 'bash bridge_helper.sh fingerprint-rotate <bridge>' to promote latest candidate row to baseline (also future-cycle scope)

side_artifacts_to_clean: /Users/ghost/core/nexus/state/bridge_schema_fingerprint.tsv (will be lazily recreated; safe to rm)
